title: (No AI) DoorDash launches a new ‘Tasks’ app that pays couriers to submit videos to train AI

title: (No AI) Adobe’s AI image generator can now be trained on your own art
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![An illustrated image representing Adobe’s Custom Firefly AI Model public beta.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Firefly-Custom-Models-Hero-Image.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Just feed your images into the bot and it’ll try to mimic specific styles and aesthetics. | Image: Adobe

Adobe is launching customizable AI image generators that can mimic specific artistic styles and character designs. The Firefly Custom Models are available in public beta starting today, allowing creators and brands to train a model on their own assets to ensure generated images follow a consistent aesthetic for characters, illustrations, and photography.

The tool aims to streamline workflows for teams and creators that need to produce high volumes of content, providing a reusable foundation that preserves visual consistency across multiple projects, instead of having to start from scratch each time. Adobe says that custom models can help pr …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/897243/adobe-firefly-ai-custom-models-image-public-beta)
contentSnippet:
Just feed your images into the bot and it’ll try to mimic specific styles and aesthetics. | Image: Adobe	

Adobe is launching customizable AI image generators that can mimic specific artistic styles and character designs. The Firefly Custom Models are available in public beta starting today, allowing creators and brands to train a model on their own assets to ensure generated images follow a consistent aesthetic for characters, illustrations, and photography.
The tool aims to streamline workflows for teams and creators that need to produce high volumes of content, providing a reusable foundation that preserves visual consistency across multiple projects, instead of having to start from scratch each time. Adobe says that custom models can help pr …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Fitbit’s AI health coach will soon be able to read your medical records
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Fitbit_STK151_01.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Would you share your medical records with a personal trainer? How about a virtual one? Google, which this week [announced](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/devices/fitbit/fitbit-personal-health-coach-updates-2026/) it is giving [Fitbit's AI health coach](https://www.theverge.com/hands-on/761965/fitbits-ai-health-coach-is-the-first-i-might-actually-be-interested-in) the ability to read your medical records, is hoping the answer is yes, following rivals like [Amazon](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/892532/amazon-is-expanding-access-to-its-health-ai-agent), [OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/857640/openai-launches-chatgpt-health-connect-medical-records), and [Microsoft](https://www.theverge.com/tech/893594/microsoft-copilot-health-launch) in betting that users are willing to trade their most sensitive data in exchange for more personalized health advice.

Starting next month in preview, US Fitbit users will be able to link their medical records to the Fitbit app. That medical data - such as lab results, medications, and visit history - will, alongside wearable data, help Fitbit's [AI-powered health …](https://www.theverge.com/gadgets/876692/fitbit-ai-health-coach-public-preview-ios)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/897250/fitbits-ai-health-coach-reads-medical-records)
contentSnippet:
Would you share your medical records with a personal trainer? How about a virtual one? Google, which this week announced it is giving Fitbit's AI health coach the ability to read your medical records, is hoping the answer is yes, following rivals like Amazon, OpenAI, and Microsoft in betting that users are willing to trade their most sensitive data in exchange for more personalized health advice. 
Starting next month in preview, US Fitbit users will be able to link their medical records to the Fitbit app. That medical data - such as lab results, medications, and visit history - will, alongside wearable data, help Fitbit's AI-powered health  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Lily Hay Newman, Matt Burgess
title: (No AI) Signal’s Creator Is Helping Encrypt Meta AI
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman, Matt Burgess
content: Moxie Marlinspike says the technology powering his encrypted AI chatbot, Confer, will be integrated into Meta AI. The move could help protect the AI conversations of millions of people.
contentSnippet: Moxie Marlinspike says the technology powering his encrypted AI chatbot, Confer, will be integrated into Meta AI. The move could help protect the AI conversations of millions of people.
